_invariant: All edges must connect exactly one token node and one protocol node. No token-token or protocol-protocol edges.

_policy.1_attribution: Count a token at the ONE place it physically rests. Never double-count.
_policy.2_cutoff: Include a node/edge only if it holds >= 0.3% of token supply OR >= $10M. Dust excluded.
_policy.3_simple_edges: Keep edges simple. Push all detail (markets, vaults, pools) to the drill-down fields (top_markets / top_pools / funding_vaults).

4_vaults.deploys_traceable: Vault forwards token to a known same-chain protocol (e.g. Spark). -> Count at that LEAF protocol. Vault is NOT its own node; list it in top_markets.funding_vaults (shown in drill-down).
4_vaults.holds_directly: Vault keeps the token in its own contract (balanceOf>0). -> Vault becomes its own node (only if >= 0.3%).
4_vaults.transformed: Token changed (WETH->stETH). -> Follow the NEW token's node.
4_vaults.cross_chain: Token left the chain (->Arbitrum/Katana). -> Model as a bridge.
4_vaults.nested_vault: Vault into another vault. -> Resolve one more hop.
4_vaults.opaque_custody: Only MPC/CeDeFi (Ethena-style) is truly untraceable. -> label 'custody (unknown)', confidence LOW.

_policy.5_wrapper: Basket wrapper (eBTC / BoringVault) HOLDS the token: edge_type=mint_backing + fill the edge.mint_backing block. The issued token (eBTC) is its OWN token node; token-token links recovered via 2-hop through the issuer protocol. (Node type is 'wrapper'; edge block is 'mint_backing' - do not confuse them.)
_policy.6_bridges: Recorded as token-node metadata (not separate protocol nodes), with mechanism + dest chains.
_policy.7_cex: Group an exchange's many addresses into ONE node via labels (only for direct holders, not vault destinations).
_policy.8_provenance: confidence HIGH only for on-chain-measured values; off-chain/claimed capped at MEDIUM/LOW. Always stamp snapshot_block.
_policy.9_cdp_is_wrapper: A CDP (Maker->DAI, Liquity->BOLD, Aave->GHO, Curve->crvUSD) is a multi-collateral wrapper: stablecoin --issued_by--> issuer; each collateral --cdp_collateral--> issuer. 'X backed by Y' recovered via 2-hop through the issuer. Stablecoin price usually PEG_ASSUMED ($1).
_policy.10_pass_through: Vault forwarding is recorded in edge.pass_through (metadata, not counted). A transformed token hands off to its OWN graph; the destination protocol (e.g. Lido) is a node THERE, not in this token's graph.

metadata._note_wrappers: If this token is a wrapper/derivative (eBTC, PT-LBTC, sUSDe...), it gets its own token node and connects to its issuer protocol via mint_backing edge. Cross-token relationships are inferred via 2-hop traversal through shared protocol.

edge._invariant_endpoints: source MUST be token:* and target MUST be protocol:* (or vice versa for clarity, but always bipartite).

classification._note_roles: A token can play multiple roles in the same protocol simultaneously (e.g. WBTC in Morpho = collateral_isolated AND loan_asset). One edge per (token, protocol) pair; roles[] holds the per-role breakdown with its own amount.

roles.edge_type: collateral | collateral_isolated | cdp_collateral | loan_asset | deposit_supply | lp_pair | mint_backing | issued_by

classification.venue_type: market | isolated_market | vault | pool | cdp | psm

oracle.oracle_type: MARKET | EXCHANGE_RATE | NAV | PEG_ASSUMED | ORACLE_FREE | NONE

cdp_risk._note: Only for CDP venues (Maker/Sky, Liquity, GHO, crvUSD, f(x)). A CDP = multi-collateral wrapper that mints a stablecoin.

pass_through._note: Where a vault FORWARDED the token (balanceOf=0 outflows). METADATA ONLY - never re-counted; the token is counted once at THIS node. For 'transformed', 'to' points to the new token's node (handoff to that token's graph; the dest protocol like Lido appears as a node THERE).

items.status: resolved | transformed | nested | crosschain | opaque | idle
